slides.title: [Company Name]
slides.subtitle: [One-line company description — what you do, for whom, with what unique angle]
slides.notes: Cover slide. Say nothing — let the visual land first. Then introduce yourself in 2 sentences max.

slides.title: [The core problem in one crisp, urgent sentence]

items.label: [Dimension of the problem — scale or frequency]
[The cost or loss this problem causes annually]

slides.notes: Lead with pain, not product. Make the problem feel real and urgent. Use a specific statistic for each point. If you can show a human story, do it here.

slides.title: Introducing [Product / Platform Name]
slides.subtitle: [One-sentence positioning: We are the [category] that [unique mechanism] so that [customer outcome].]
items: [Differentiator 2 — the workflow or integration that saves time]
slides.notes: Solution slide must be 3× more concise than the problem slide. One sentence positioning. Three proof points. No feature laundry list.

slides.title: A $[X]B market ripe for disruption
slides.notes: Investors want to see a large market with a credible path to owning a slice. Show you understand the difference between TAM, SAM, and SOM. Never top-down only — add a bottoms-up calculation.

slides.title: Traction that tells the story
slides.quote: "[Best customer quote — ideally a measurable outcome in their own words]"
slides.notes: Real numbers only. If early stage, use engagement metrics or pilot results. A great customer quote can replace two slides of claims. Show the growth curve, not just the peak.

slides.title: The team built to win this

members.name: [Founder Name]
members.bio: [1–2 lines: relevant prior experience, credential, or founder-market fit signal]

members.name: [Founder Name]
members.bio: [1–2 lines: technical background, relevant build history, domain expertise]

members.name: [Advisor Name]
members.bio: [Why this advisor matters — their specific network or knowledge adds what?]

slides.notes: Lead with founder-market fit. Why are YOU the right people to solve THIS problem? Mention prior exits, relevant domain time, or unique insight. Advisors should explain why they're relevant, not just impressive.

slides.title: The Ask
milestones: [Milestone 1 — what this round gets you in 18 months]
[Milestone 2 — the metric that unlocks the Series B]
slides.notes: Be specific about the ask. Explain what milestones this round buys. Investors want to know: what does the next round look like, and what do you need to get there?

slides.title: [Founder Name]
slides.subtitle: [email@company.com] · [website.com]
slides.notes: End with your contact. Keep this on screen during Q&A. Have a one-pager ready to leave behind.
